LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DENNIS MCNAMAR'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND TO DISMISS COMPLAINT FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION (CCP 481.10) IS CONTINUED TO DECEMBER 14, 2018, TO PERMIT PLAINTIFF TO CONDUCT THE JURISDICTIONAL DISCOVERY SET OUT ON PAGE 5 OF HIS OPPOSITION BRIEF. (SEE HEALTHMARKETS, INC. V. SUP. CT. (2009) 171 CAL.APP.4TH 1160, 1173.) ANY SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEFS SHALL BE FILED ON OR BEFORE DECEMBER 3, 2018. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING. THE PROPOSED ORDER IS TO BE IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, EMAIL: MTORREANO@SFTC.ORG. =(302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And To Dismiss Complaint For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction (Ccp 481.10)
Off Calendar Dec-14-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT DENNIS MCNAMARA's Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And To Dismiss Complaint For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction (Ccp 481.10) is OFF CALENDAR. Dismissal filed on December 4, 2018.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/HEK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-19-2018 Continued To Jan-30-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And To Dismiss Complaint For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction (Ccp 481.10)
Off Calendar Sep-14-2018 Continued To Dec-14-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance by any party or counsel.

DEFENDANT DENNIS MCNAMAR'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND TO DISMISS COMPLAINT FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION (CCP 481.10).

There being no appearance, the Court adopted the tentative ruling:

Dennis McNamara's motion to quash for lack of personal jurisdiction is CONTINUED to December 14, 2018, to permit plaintiff to conduct the jurisdictional discovery set out on page 5 of his opposition brief. (See HealthMarkets, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 1160, 1173.) Any supplemental briefs shall be filed on or before December 3, 2018. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. The proposed order is to be in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: mtorreano@sftc.org. =(302/RBU)
